chapter: Chapter 5: Tales by Dots and Lines

questions.question: Define mean and explain why it is called a measure of central tendency.
questions.answer: The mean is the sum of all observations divided by the number of observations. It represents the central value of the dataset and hence is called a measure of central tendency.

questions.question: Explain how the mean represents the balance point of data.
questions.answer: The mean acts like a balancing point where the total distance of observations on one side of the mean equals the total distance on the other side.

questions.question: Explain what happens to the mean when a new value greater than the mean is added to a dataset.
questions.answer: When a value greater than the mean is added, the mean increases because the balance of the dataset shifts toward the larger value.

questions.question: Explain the concept of median with an example.
questions.answer: The median is the middle value of a dataset when the values are arranged in ascending order. For example, in the set {3, 5, 7, 9, 11}, the median is 7.

questions.question: Explain how the median changes when a new value is added to the dataset.
questions.answer: If a value greater than the median is added, the median may increase. If a value smaller than the median is added, the median may decrease depending on the dataset.

questions.question: Explain the method of calculating mean using frequency tables.
questions.answer: To calculate mean using frequency tables, multiply each value by its frequency, add the results, and divide by the total frequency.

questions.question: Explain how the median can be determined from a frequency table.
questions.answer: Arrange data in order, compute cumulative frequencies, and identify the middle position to determine the median value.

questions.question: Explain the role of spreadsheets in data analysis.
questions.answer: Spreadsheets allow users to organize data in rows and columns and perform calculations like sum and average using formulas.

questions.question: Explain the structure of a spreadsheet.
questions.answer: A spreadsheet is composed of rows and columns forming cells where data and formulas can be entered for calculations.

questions.question: Explain what a line graph is and when it is used.
questions.answer: A line graph represents data using points connected by line segments and is commonly used to show changes over time.

questions.question: Explain how a line graph helps interpret temperature trends.
questions.answer: A line graph allows us to observe patterns and variations in temperature over time by connecting plotted data points.

questions.question: Explain why line graphs are useful for showing changes over time.
questions.answer: Line graphs show trends clearly by connecting data points, making it easier to track increases and decreases across time.

questions.question: Explain the concept of dot plots.
questions.answer: A dot plot is a simple graphical display where dots represent data points on a number line.

questions.question: Explain how dot plots help understand data distribution.
questions.answer: Dot plots visually show clusters, gaps, and frequency of values in a dataset.

questions.question: Explain how rainfall patterns can be represented using line graphs.
questions.answer: Rainfall data across months can be plotted as points and connected to show seasonal patterns.

questions.question: Explain the steps involved in interpreting a graph.
questions.answer: First identify what information the graph shows, then observe patterns and trends, and finally draw conclusions from the data.

questions.question: Explain how graphs help in decision making.
questions.answer: Graphs simplify complex datasets and allow quick understanding of trends, helping people make informed decisions.

questions.question: Explain the importance of data visualization.
questions.answer: Data visualization helps represent information clearly using graphs, charts, and diagrams.

questions.question: Explain how graphs can represent large datasets effectively.
questions.answer: Graphs condense large amounts of data into visual patterns that are easier to interpret.

questions.question: Explain the difference between mean and median.
questions.answer: Mean is the average of all values while median is the middle value of an ordered dataset.

questions.question: Explain how statistical measures help in analysing real-life data.
questions.answer: Statistical measures like mean and median summarize datasets and help understand patterns.

questions.question: Explain how trends can be identified using graphs.
questions.answer: Trends are identified by observing whether graph lines move upward, downward, or remain stable.

questions.question: Explain how the mean changes if every value in the dataset increases by a constant number.
questions.answer: If every value increases by a constant number, the mean also increases by the same number.

questions.question: Explain how multiplying every value in a dataset affects the mean.
questions.answer: If every value in a dataset is multiplied by a number, the mean is also multiplied by that number.

questions.question: Explain how data stories help understand statistical information.
questions.answer: Data stories use graphs and explanations to present data patterns in a meaningful and engaging way.
